```bash
for f in $(ls -1 /testbed/*.php 2>/dev/null | sort); do echo -n "$f: "; wc -l < "$f"; done
```
raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that counts lines in each *.php file sorted by file in /testbed. Use find or ls. Use wc -l. For each file sorted. So: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 -I{} sh -c 'echo -n "{}: "; wc -l < "{}"' . But simpler: for f in $(ls -1 /testbed/*.php | sort); do echo -n "$f: "; wc -l < "$f"; done. Provide one command. Use bash.
